perturbation.original_text: Distributor agrees that the Products, including the name Snotarator ™ Nasal Aspirator (the "Brand") are the sole property of the Company and Distributor has no interest whatsoever in such Brand and Products, and Distributor shall use the Brand and Products only for so long as the rights granted hereby remains in full force and effect.
perturbation.changed_text: Distributor agrees that the Products, including the name Snotarator ™ Nasal Aspirator (the "Brand") are the sole property of the Distributor and Distributor has no interest whatsoever in such Brand and Products, and Distributor shall use the Brand and Products only for so long as the rights granted hereby remains in full force and effect.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by stating that the 'Brand' and 'Products' are the sole property of the 'Distributor', when the agreement initially states, and implies throughout, they are the Company's property. This creates uncertainty on who owns these items, making the contract unenforceable as it is directly contradicts the initial statement, implying ownership and rights to the Distributor when the intention, initially was to provide them distribution rights, but not ownership. Specifically, this contradicts with Section 4.02 'Trademark Infringement', which reaffirms the Company's ownership.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Company has registered the mark Snotarator ™ in the United States. Distributor agrees it will not at any time, either during the term of this Agreement or thereafter, use the Brand, Products or Snotarator™ mark in any manner that might infringe upon the Company's ownership rights to same directly or indirectly. Distributor shall indemnify and hold the Company and its affiliates harmless from and against any and all claims, judgments, costs, awards, expenses (including reasonable attorney fees) and liabilities of every kind arising from Distributor's fault or negligence in its use of the Snotarator ™ mark and in the marketing and distribution of the Products within the Territory.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text. The changed text states the 'Brand' and 'Products' are the sole property of the 'Distributor'. The contradicted text states 'The Company has registered the mark Snotarator ™ in the United States' and Distributor agrees not to infringe upon the Company's ownership rights. The change introduces a direct conflict regarding ownership of the brand, creating a strong in-text contradiction and making enforcement unclear.

perturbation.original_text: Purchase orders and other administrative notices may be sent by facsimile transmission or regular mail.
perturbation.changed_text: Purchase orders and other administrative notices must be sent exclusively by international courier.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates a contradiction by modifying the method of sending 'purchase orders and other administrative notices.' Originally, it allowed for flexibility with 'facsimile transmission or regular mail.' The revision restricts the options to 'exclusively by international courier,' contradicting the original provision and imposing a potentially unreasonable and costly burden on the parties. This contradicts with section 7.01, which further elaborates acceptable methods including regular mail.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Notice or Communication. Any notice or communication required or permitted hereunder shall be in writing and shall be sent by international courier or registered mail, return receipt requested, postage prepaid and addressed to the addresses set forth below or to such changed address as any party entitled to notice shall have communicated in writing to the other party. Purchase orders and other administrative notices may be sent by facsimile transmission or regular mail. Notices and communications to Company shall be sent to: Snotarator LLC 2591 Dallas Parkway Suite 102 Frisco, Texas 75034 Notices and communications to Distributor shall be sent to address shown on first page of this Agreement. Any notices or communications to either party hereunder shall be deemed to have been given when deposited in the mail, addressed to the then current address of such party.
perturbation.justification: The change modifies the acceptable methods for sending 'purchase orders and other administrative notices.' The original text allowed 'facsimile transmission or regular mail,' while the changed text mandates 'exclusively by international courier.' This creates a direct conflict within the contradicted section, which explicitly states 'Purchase orders and other administrative notices may be sent by facsimile transmission or regular mail.' The change eliminates previously allowed methods, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction. This falls under 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' because acceptable methods are being used interchangeably, leading to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: The relationship between the parties established by this Agreement shall be solely that of vendor and vendee and all rights and powers not expressly granted to the Distributor are expressly reserved to the Company.
perturbation.changed_text: The relationship between the parties established by this Agreement shall be that of partners, with all rights and powers shared equally, unless otherwise specified.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a fundamental contradiction. The original text clearly defines the relationship as 'vendor and vendee,' explicitly reserving all ungranted rights to the Company. The modified version redefines the relationship as 'partners' with equal rights, which directly contradicts the originally intended vendor-vendee relationship. This contradicts section 9.02 where it elaborates on the Independence of parties and the Distributor acting as an independent contractor, therefore the term partner, contradicts this aspect and creates an in-text contradiction within the document
perturbation.contradicted_text: Independence of Parties. Nothing contained in this Agreement shall be construed to make the Distributor the agent for the Company for any purpose, and neither party hereto shall have any right whatsoever to incur any liabilities or obligations on behalf or binding upon the other party. The Distributor specifically agrees that it shall have no power or authority to represent the Company in any manner; that it will solicit orders for Products as an independent contractor in accordance with the terms of this Agreement; and that it will not at any time represent the Company in any manner; that it will solicit orders for Products as an independent contractor in accordance with the terms of this Agreement; and that it will not at any time represent orally or in writing to any person or corporation or other business entity that it has any right, power or authority not expressly granted by this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text redefines the relationship between the parties from 'vendor and vendee' to 'partners' with shared rights. This directly contradicts the 'Independence of Parties' clause, which explicitly states the Distributor is an independent contractor, not an agent or partner, and cannot incur liabilities or obligations on behalf of the Company. The term 'partners' implies a level of agency and shared liability that is incompatible with the expressly stated independence of the Distributor in the contradicted section.
